title: Mrs Dalloway
description: A day in the life of Clarissa Dalloway as she prepares for a party, exploring themes of mental illness, time, and post-war society.

title: To the Lighthouse
description: A philosophical exploration of family dynamics, memory, and the passage of time centered around visits to a summer home in the Hebrides.

title: Orlando
description: A fantastical biography spanning four centuries, following a poet who changes sex from man to woman.

title: A Room of One's Own
description: An extended essay on women and fiction, arguing that a woman must have money and a room of her own to write.

title: The Waves
description: An experimental novel following six characters from childhood to old age through interior monologues.

title: Ulysses
description: A modernist retelling of Homer's Odyssey set in Dublin, following Leopold Bloom through a single day.

title: A Portrait of the Artist as a Young Man
description: A semi-autobiographical novel depicting the intellectual and artistic development of Stephen Dedalus.

title: Dubliners
description: A collection of fifteen short stories depicting Irish middle class life in Dublin in the early 20th century.

title: The Metamorphosis
description: The story of Gregor Samsa who wakes one morning to find himself transformed into a giant insect.

title: The Trial
description: A nightmarish tale of a man arrested and prosecuted by a remote, inaccessible authority, with the nature of his crime revealed neither to him nor to the reader.

title: The Castle
description: A land surveyor struggles to gain access to the mysterious authorities of a castle who govern the village.

title: The Waste Land
description: A long modernist poem depicting the disillusionment and despair of the post-World War I generation.

title: The Love Song of J. Alfred Prufrock
description: A dramatic monologue presenting the thoughts of a middle-aged man paralyzed by indecision.

title: Four Quartets
description: Four interlinked meditations exploring time, perspective, humanity, and salvation.

title: In Search of Lost Time
description: A seven-volume novel exploring themes of memory, time, and consciousness through the narrator's recollections.

title: The Sun Also Rises
description: A novel about a group of American and British expatriates traveling from Paris to Pamplona for the running of the bulls.

title: A Farewell to Arms
description: A novel about an American ambulance driver in the Italian army during World War I and his love affair with a nurse.

title: The Old Man and the Sea
description: The story of an aging Cuban fisherman who struggles with a giant marlin in the Gulf Stream.

title: The Great Gatsby
description: A portrait of the Jazz Age and its excesses, following mysterious millionaire Jay Gatsby and his obsession with Daisy Buchanan.

title: Tender Is the Night
description: A novel about the rise and fall of Dick Diver, a promising young psychiatrist and his wife Nicole.

title: The Sound and the Fury
description: A novel told from four different perspectives, chronicling the decline of the Compson family.

title: As I Lay Dying
description: A novel narrated by 15 different characters as they transport a body for burial.

title: Three Lives
description: Three stories exploring the inner lives of three working-class women.

title: The Autobiography of Alice B. Toklas
description: An autobiography of Gertrude Stein written as though by her partner Alice B. Toklas.

title: The Cantos
description: An epic, unfinished long poem incorporating classical mythology, economics, and history.

title: Dark Horse Records: The Story of George Harrison's Post-Beatles Record Label
